ux_analysis_and_recommendations.1_optimal_step_order: Home (Search) -> Car Selection -> Trip Recap (Route Map & Premium Perks) -> Inline Passenger Details (Auth) -> Payment (Stripe) -> Confirmation. This sequence builds value and commitment before introducing friction.
ux_analysis_and_recommendations.2_map_recap_before_after_registration: BEFORE. The 'Commitment and Consistency' principle dictates that users should see the full value (exact route, vehicle, price, premium amenities) to build desire. Asking for registration before the recap causes high bounce rates as users haven't validated their purchase yet.
ux_analysis_and_recommendations.3_registration_modal_vs_page: Neither as a traditional barrier. Registration should be masked as a 'Passenger Details' step within an Inline Split-Pane Checkout or an accordion. On mobile, use a seamless Bottom Sheet. Do not use generic centered modals that block the user's view of what they are booking. The goal is a 'Guest Checkout' feel that registers them seamlessly via the C# API.
ux_analysis_and_recommendations.4_presenting_premium_value: In Car Selection: Use minimalist duotone icons (e.g., Phosphor Icons) for Water, 60min Wait, Meet & Greet with Name Sign on each car card. In the Recap/Checkout: Dedicate a prominent 'Included Benefits' Bento Grid block with a subtle border/glow (The 'Tracing Beam' surface strategy). This reassures the premium price point without adding steps.
ux_analysis_and_recommendations.5_combine_registration_and_payment: YES. Combine them into a single-page 'Accordion' or 'Split-Pane' checkout. Left pane: Step 1 (Contact/Auth), Step 2 (Flight Details), Step 3 (Payment). Right pane: Sticky Order Summary. This prevents the psychological fatigue of multiple page reloads.
ux_analysis_and_recommendations.6_mobile_first_recommendations: 1. Use 'Bottom Sheets' (Drawer) for car details and auth instead of full-page navigation. 2. Sticky bottom CTA ('Book for €XX') during the entire checkout scroll. 3. Auto-trigger numeric keypads for phone and Stripe fields. 4. Collapsible/mini-map to preserve vertical space (max-h-[30vh] for map on mobile).

typography.heading_rules: Use Playfair Display for marketing headings, large titles, and luxury positioning. Apply 'tracking-tight' to headings. DO NOT use generic fonts like Inter for headings. Keep it elegant.
typography.body_rules: Use Manrope for all UI, form fields, buttons, and data tables. It offers excellent readability for numbers (prices, times, flight numbers).
typography.font_weights: Use distinct contrast: 'font-light' for elegant subheadings, 'font-semibold' for primary data and prices.

colors.rules: Premium Dark Theme (Jewel & Luxury + Performance Pro). Use the Emerald Green ONLY for primary actions (Search, Select, Pay). DO NOT use gradients for backgrounds. Use 'Tinted Grey' (slate/gray) rather than pure black. Never use pure #000000.

spacing_and_layout.general_spacing: Generous Spacing Philosophy. Use p-6, p-8, p-12 to create breathing room, especially in the Car Selection cards and Checkout summary.
spacing_and_layout.grid_system: Bento Grid for the Trip Recap. 12-column grid on Desktop. 1-column mobile-first.
spacing_and_layout.surface_strategies: Glassmorphism for the Sticky Nav (bg-gray-900/70 backdrop-blur-xl border-b border-white/10). Solid backgrounds with 1px border-gray-800 for Car Cards. Do NOT use heavy box-shadows in dark mode; use borders and subtle inner glows.

components.forms: Use Shadcn Form, Input, Select. Customize inputs with h-12 or h-14, rounded-none or rounded-md (slight), border-gray-700, bg-gray-900. Focus state: ring-1 ring-emerald-500.
components.car_card: Vertical or Horizontal layout depending on device. Large WebP image of the car, Playfair Display title, Manrope price (e.g., €85). Minimalist icon row for specs (Luggage x2, Pax x3).
components.drawers_and_modals: Use Shadcn Drawer (Bottom Sheet) for Mobile interactions. Use Dialog for Desktop if necessary, but prefer inline split-panes for Checkout.
components.buttons: Shadcn Button. Primary: bg-emerald-500 text-white hover:bg-emerald-600. Large touch targets (min-h-[48px]). Use 'group' and group-hover for internal icon translation (arrow moving right).

visual_enhancers.icons: Phosphor Icons (Duotone weight). Used for amenities (water, wifi, meet and greet, luggage).
visual_enhancers.map: Google Maps with 'Dark/Night' JSON styling to blend seamlessly into the #0A0E17 background. Hide POIs to keep the route clean.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle noise/grain texture overlaid on the Hero section to break the 'digital flatness' and feel cinematic.

motion.page_transitions: Framer Motion. Fade and slight slide-up (y: 10) for page entry.
motion.interactions: Hover state on Car Cards: slight scale-up of car image, border color shift to emerald-500/50. Hover on buttons: bg-color shift + slight transform.
motion.list_stagger: When loading Car Selection, stagger the entry of the car cards.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over images has a dark gradient overlay. All text must pass WCAG AA (Manrope handles this well in small sizes).
accessibility.focus_states: All form inputs MUST have explicit focus:ring-emerald-500 to guide the user through the checkout flow.
accessibility.aria_labels: Add aria-labels to all icon-only buttons (like map zoom, or edit selection).

testing_attributes.rules: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid. Examples: data-testid='search-transfer-btn', data-testid='select-car-regular', data-testid='checkout-submit-btn', data-testid='passenger-email-input'.

hero_backgrounds.description: Premium chauffeur opening door for client

3d_libraries.recommendation: None for the core booking flow. Performance and conversion are paramount. Use clean 2D UI with Framer Motion for micro-interactions and a highly stylized Google Map API integration for the route visual.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Restructure the routing to support the new flow: Home -> CarSelection -> Recap -> Checkout (Auth + Payment combined) -> Confirmation.
2. On CarSelection.js, clicking 'Select' should immediately route to the Recap page, passing the selected car data. DO NOT trigger auth here.
3. Build a Trip Recap component featuring a stylized dark-mode map, the selected car, price, and premium amenities (Water, 60m Wait, Name Sign) prominently displayed.

4. Refactor Auth/Checkout: Create a single Split-Pane view. Left side is a multi-step accordion (Step 1: Contact Details, Step 2: Payment). Entering Contact Details should handle the C# API registration/login transparently under the hood.
5. Ensure Shadcn forms are used with Manrope font for all inputs and data displays.
6. Apply the Playfair Display font globally for all primary headings.
7. Implement strict mobile-first bottom-sheets (Drawers) for the car details and mobile checkout steps.
8. Add data-testid attributes to all buttons, inputs, and major interaction points.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Do not converge toward generic outputs. Use the defined typography (Playfair Display + Manrope).
Never use pure black or pure white; always use the tinted palette provided (#0A0E17 for background).
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Avoid cramped layouts.
Do not use generic centered layouts. Use optical alignment and bento grids.
Create depth with borders (border-white/10) and subtle glows instead of heavy shadows in dark mode.
Components MUST use named exports, pages MUST use default exports.
NEVER use emojis for icons. Use the specified Phosphor Icons library.
Do NOT apply universal transitions (transition: all).
